title: Alsine procumbens
intro: Alsine procumbens, also known as creeping pearlwort, is a low-growing, mat-forming perennial plant native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its purported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Alsine procumbens include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Creeping pearlwort is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs to support overall health and well-being.
